requests.title: Google Docs / Drive Documentation Read Access
requests.purpose: Inspect UMH, EOS, CreatorOS, and LyfeOS documentation to identify current vs outdated docs, map product claims, and extract architecture plans for cross-source reconciliation.
what_will_be_read: Product specifications, architecture docs, implementation plans
what_will_not_happen: No documents will be modified, deleted, or created
No content will be copied to external systems
No sharing permissions will be changed
No comments will be added to documents
No documents will be auto-canonized without operator approval
inferences_that_may_be_made: Which documents are current vs stale based on last-modified dates
Which product claims in docs match or diverge from code state
Which architecture decisions documented in Docs are implemented vs planned
Cross-reference between Doc content and /opt/OS code
least_privilege_options: Full read access to all projection-related Drive folders
Read access to specific named documents only
Metadata-only access (titles, dates, folder structure — no content)
Operator-guided document selection (tell UMH which docs to read)

requests.title: GitHub Repository Read Access
requests.purpose: Inspect repository structure, branch state, committed source code, and PR history to compare committed code with local/device sources and identify stale or divergent source.
what_will_be_read: Committed source code for EOS, CreatorOS, LyfeOS repositories
what_will_not_happen: No branches will be created, deleted, or force-pushed
No PRs will be created, merged, or closed
No code will be pushed to any repository
No repository settings will be changed
No issues will be created or modified
inferences_that_may_be_made: Which repos contain which projection source
Whether GitHub source matches /opt/OS/saas or Windows /dev source
Which branches are stale vs active
Whether committed code represents latest product state
least_privilege_options: Full read access to all user repositories
Read access to specific named repositories only
Metadata-only access (repo names, branch names, file trees — no content)

requests.title: Windows Beast /dev Filesystem Read Access
requests.purpose: Inspect Trinity app source trees on the Windows Beast to map EOS, CreatorOS, and LyfeOS files and compare with /opt/OS/saas.
what_will_be_read: Source file listings per app directory
Git status and branch state per app
what_will_not_happen: No files will be modified, deleted, or created
No files will be copied to VPS without explicit approval
No git operations will be performed (no commits, no pushes)
No npm/package operations will be run
No builds will be triggered
No private/sensitive directories outside /dev will be accessed
inferences_that_may_be_made: Which Trinity app source lives where
Which apps have frontend vs backend vs both
Which apps are actively developed vs dormant
Package dependency overlap between Trinity apps
least_privilege_options: Full read access to /dev directory tree
Read access to specific app directories only (e.g., /dev/eos, /dev/creatoros)
Metadata-only access (directory listing + package.json — no source code)

requests.title: Device Filesystem Source Discovery
requests.purpose: Identify other local app, design, or build artifacts on connected devices that may contain projection-relevant source or documentation.
what_will_be_read: Top-level directory listings on known devices
what_will_not_happen: No private/personal directories will be read
No sensitive files (credentials, keys, personal documents) will be accessed
No files will be modified or copied
No network services will be accessed
Scope is strictly development-related directories
inferences_that_may_be_made: Whether design assets exist alongside code
Whether additional build artifacts exist beyond /dev
Whether other devices have projection-related source
least_privilege_options: Skip entirely — rely on operator-provided inventory
